## In excess — spiritual self-deception, mistaking the map

Severe Bhrantidarshana creates deeply false views, mistaking progress, spiritual delusions, fixed wrong understandings that prevent correction.

Somatic (body): Misreading body's signals consistently, harmful practices believed beneficial.

Emotional current: Mistaking emotional states (calling agitation peace), emotional self-deception.

Cognitive map: Deep false views about reality, spiritual delusions, mistaking experiences, fundamental misunderstanding of path.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul wanders a self-built map; spiritual delusion — mistaking imagination for attainment — is among the most dangerous obstacles precisely because it feels like progress.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Over-corrected — hypervigilance and anxiety from relentless self-monitoring, the inability to ever relax

Excessive vigilance without rest becomes hypervigilance, anxiety, inability to ever relax. Some natural attention/inattention rhythm is healthy.

Somatic (body): Hypervigilance creating tension, inability to relax, stress-related illness.

Emotional current: Anxious over-attention, inability to be at ease, emotional rigidity from constant vigilance.

Cognitive map: Hypervigilant mind, anxiety, inability to rest mental attention, exhaustion from constant focus.

Inner presence (psychic): Vigilance hardens into hypervigilance; terrified of slipping, the soul monitors itself so relentlessly that it can never rest in the ease that is the point.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — sustained, vigilant attention

When Pramada is overcome, the yogi maintains constant mindful awareness. There is steady attention without forgetting one's purpose and practice.

Somatic (body): Mindful physical presence, careful attention to body, healthy alertness, careful actions.

Emotional current: Emotional mindfulness, careful attention to feelings, freedom from emotional carelessness.

Cognitive map: Constant mental awareness, careful attention, mindfulness maintained, no negligence.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul keeps its thread; steady mindful attention is the continuity through which all practice deepens.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — chronic inattention, the dropped thread

Excessive Pramada is habitual carelessness—forgetting practice constantly, never coming back to awareness, mindlessness as a way of life.

Somatic (body): Careless physical action, accidents, neglect of body, mindless living.

Emotional current: Emotional carelessness, hurting others through negligence, emotional mindlessness.

Cognitive map: Chronic mindlessness, no attention, mind always wandering, no return to awareness.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul keeps forgetting itself; carelessness lets hard-won awareness slip again and again, the thread dropped almost as soon as it is picked up.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — credulous certainty that never examines

Complete absence of all doubt becomes blind faith, gullibility, inability to discriminate, vulnerability to false teachers and ideas.

Somatic (body): Blind following of harmful practices, physical danger from no discrimination.

Emotional current: Emotional gullibility, vulnerability to manipulation, blind devotion to wrong figures.

Cognitive map: Blind faith, gullibility, absence of healthy critical thinking, vulnerability to deception.

Inner presence (psychic): Doubt collapses into blind credulity; without the healthy check of discernment, the soul becomes prey to whatever certainty is offered it.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — honest questioning that seeks truth

When Samshaya is overcome through faith (shraddha) and experience, the yogi practices with conviction. Healthy questioning remains, but corrosive doubt is gone.

Somatic (body): Body practiced with conviction, healthy commitment to practice, physical confidence.

Emotional current: Emotional confidence in path, faith from experience, freedom from corrosive doubt.

Cognitive map: Mental conviction supporting practice, clarity about path, healthy critical thinking without paralysis.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul questions honestly and then trusts; doubt resolved into conviction frees the energy that endless skepticism would consume.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — doubt that paralyzes commitment

Excessive Samshaya becomes chronic doubt that undermines all action, paralysis through skepticism, inability to commit to anything.

Somatic (body): Inability to sustain practice, body in inconsistent action, no commitment to physical routines.

Emotional current: Emotional instability from doubt, inability to trust feelings, anxiety from uncertainty.

Cognitive map: Paralyzing skepticism, mental inability to commit, constant questioning that prevents action.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is paralyzed at the threshold; chronic doubt undermines every step, the faculty meant to discern truth refusing to let it land.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — (the keen alertness in which dullness finds no foothold)

Styana as obstacle means dullness present. When absent, the mind is alert—this is the desired state.

Somatic (body): Alert body, vital energy, healthy responsiveness.

Emotional current: Engaged emotions, enthusiasm, joy.

Cognitive map: Sharp clear mind, alert thinking, enthusiastic engagement.

Inner presence (psychic): The dullness lifts entirely — the keen, awake mind in which apathy finds no foothold, which is precisely the clarity practice exists to restore.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — a clear, willing, alert mind

When Styana is overcome, the mind is alert and enthusiastic for practice. There is mental vitality and engagement.

Somatic (body): Alert physical presence, vital energy, body that supports active engagement, healthy nervous system.

Emotional current: Enthusiasm and emotional engagement, joy in practice, emotional vitality.

Cognitive map: Mental alertness, enthusiasm for yoga, sharp clarity, engaged thinking.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul’s light reaches the surface through a clear, willing mind; enthusiasm for practice is itself a sign that the inner fire still burns.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — fog, apathy, no enthusiasm for practice

Excessive (and prolonged) Styana becomes chronic apathy, depression-like state, complete loss of motivation for practice or life.

Somatic (body): Severe lethargy, body weighed down by inertia, lack of physical energy, depression-like physical state.

Emotional current: Emotional flatness, depression, loss of all enthusiasm, emotional dullness, no joy.

Cognitive map: Severe mental fog, complete apathy, inability to think clearly, dulled cognition.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is smothered under tamas; chronic dullness is the heaviness that makes even the longing for freedom feel impossibly distant.

descriptions.deficiency:
# 3. Vyādhi — Disease

व्याधि   ·   Sutra 1.30

The nature of this aspect. Vyadhi is disease or illness—the first of nine obstacles Patanjali names that distract the mind from yoga. Physical illness disturbs mental focus and impedes practice.

## Balanced — health as the ground of practice

When the body is healthy, it serves as a fit vehicle for yoga practice. Vyadhi is overcome through proper care, lifestyle, and yogic practices.

Somatic (body): Healthy disease-free body, strong immune function, vitality, balanced physiology, body fit for practice.

Emotional current: Emotional stability supported by physical health, freedom from disease-related distress.

Cognitive map: Mental clarity from healthy body, focus undisturbed by physical complaints, capacity for practice.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul has a fit vehicle; bodily health, neither clung to nor neglected, becomes a quiet support for the inner work rather than a distraction from it.

## In excess — health-anxiety and hypochondria; identification with symptoms

Obsession with illness (excessive focus) becomes hypochondria, health anxiety, identification with sickness, life dominated by illness narrative.

Somatic (body): Hypochondria, exaggerated physical symptoms, somatization, illness identity becoming dominant.

Emotional current: Emotional identification with illness, victim mentality around disease, anxiety about health.

Cognitive map: Obsessive mental focus on health, illness anxiety, mental dominance by disease concerns.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is eclipsed by preoccupation with the body; health-anxiety turns the instrument into the obsession, the fixation itself a subtle bondage.

## When present — actual disease disrupting practice and focus

Vyadhi as obstacle means disease is interfering with practice. When fully present, it creates inability to do yoga, distraction, suffering.

Somatic (body): Active disease, body unable to practice, physical suffering, illness disrupting daily life.

Emotional current: Emotional distress from illness, depression from sickness, frustration with body.

Cognitive map: Mental distraction by physical suffering, inability to focus on practice, mind dominated by symptoms.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is asked to find its ground beyond the body precisely when the body fails; illness becomes a wall or a teacher depending on whether one identifies with the ailing form or witnesses it.

## External conduct

A balanced relationship to vyādhi treats the body as the vehicle of all practice — tended, rested, fed, and moved well enough to be forgotten. When illness genuinely strikes, it disturbs focus directly; and a mind already caught in obstacle can also become preoccupied with the body, magnifying small symptoms into a full-time concern. Its neglect-pole is the practitioner who pushes the body past its limits in the name of discipline until it breaks down and stops practice altogether.

## A day in the life

A bad cold arrives the week Maya finally established a morning sit. The congestion, the broken sleep, the low-grade ache make the cushion feel impossible, and within days the habit is gone — not abandoned by choice but dissolved by the body’s disturbance. Seeing it as vyādhi rather than as personal failure, she lets the practice shrink to two honest minutes a day until the body returns.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — health as the ground of practice

When the body is healthy, it serves as a fit vehicle for yoga practice. Vyadhi is overcome through proper care, lifestyle, and yogic practices.

Somatic (body): Healthy disease-free body, strong immune function, vitality, balanced physiology, body fit for practice.

Emotional current: Emotional stability supported by physical health, freedom from disease-related distress.

Cognitive map: Mental clarity from healthy body, focus undisturbed by physical complaints, capacity for practice.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul has a fit vehicle; bodily health, neither clung to nor neglected, becomes a quiet support for the inner work rather than a distraction from it.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — health-anxiety and hypochondria; identification with symptoms

Obsession with illness (excessive focus) becomes hypochondria, health anxiety, identification with sickness, life dominated by illness narrative.

Somatic (body): Hypochondria, exaggerated physical symptoms, somatization, illness identity becoming dominant.

Emotional current: Emotional identification with illness, victim mentality around disease, anxiety about health.

Cognitive map: Obsessive mental focus on health, illness anxiety, mental dominance by disease concerns.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is eclipsed by preoccupation with the body; health-anxiety turns the instrument into the obsession, the fixation itself a subtle bondage.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — cruelty, cold indifference, the turned-away face

Absence of Karuna creates cruelty, indifference to suffering, hardness of heart, inability to care.

Somatic (body): Closed body in presence of suffering, physical coldness, hard presence.

Emotional current: Emotional coldness, cruelty, inability to feel for sufferers, hardness of heart.

Cognitive map: Mental dismissal of others' suffering, callous thinking, rationalization for not caring.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul hardens and turns away; the closed heart is the surest sign of a self still defended against the world’s pain.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — warm, steady compassion that can act

When Karuna is established, genuine compassion arises in face of suffering. The yogi feels for others without absorption, helps wisely.

Somatic (body): Open heart center, warm physical presence with suffering others, healthy compassionate engagement.

Emotional current: Genuine compassion and warmth toward suffering, ability to help from balance, emotional generosity.

Cognitive map: Wisdom about how to alleviate suffering, clear thinking compassion, mental purity from caring.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul meets suffering with a compassion that can act — the heart open and the ground steady, mirroring the boundless mercy it is learning to embody.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — over-identification, burnout, drowning in another’s pain

Excessive Karuna becomes emotional absorption in others' pain, codependent rescuing, overwhelm from compassion, burnout.

Somatic (body): Compassion fatigue, physical exhaustion from over-helping, body absorbing others' tension.

Emotional current: Emotional overwhelm by others' suffering, codependent rescuing, emotional drowning.

Cognitive map: Mental confusion about helping vs enabling, inability to maintain perspective.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul drowns in another’s pain, losing the steadiness from which real help flows; compassion without equanimity exhausts itself.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — envy, comparison, the soured response to others’ joy

Absence of Maitri creates envy, jealousy of successful people, bitter comparison, suffering at others' joy.

Somatic (body): Tension in presence of successful others, stress from envy, physical signs of jealousy.

Emotional current: Envy and jealousy, bitterness, emotional suffering at others' success, inability to feel for happy others.

Cognitive map: Comparison thinking, mental bitterness, distorted perception of success, agitation from envy.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul contracts into the prison of comparison; envy at another’s joy is the exact measure of how separate one still feels.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — genuine gladness at another’s good fortune

When Maitri is established, a person feels genuine warmth and friendliness toward happy and successful people. Envy dissolves, mental serenity arises.

Somatic (body): Open warm physical presence with happy others, healthy nervous system, body that doesn't tense around success.

Emotional current: Genuine joy at others' happiness, warmth in relationships, freedom from envy, emotional abundance.

Cognitive map: Mental serenity, freedom from comparison thinking, clarity from non-envious mind, peaceful thinking.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul recognizes itself in another’s happiness; gladness at others’ good fortune dissolves the boundary between self and other, which is the secret the brahmavihāras exist to teach.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — ingratiating over-attachment, people-pleasing

Excessive Maitri without discernment becomes inability to address others' faults, false agreeableness, sycophancy.

Somatic (body): Physical inability to take stands, weakness in face of harm to happy others.

Emotional current: Emotional sycophancy, false agreeableness, inability to give honest feedback.

Cognitive map: Mental inability to discriminate, avoiding necessary critical assessment, false harmony.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul loses itself in the need to be liked; friendliness curdles into people-pleasing, and the self disappears into approval rather than into love.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — competitive resentment, the begrudging of others’ goodness

Absence of Mudita creates jealousy of virtue, resentment of others' spiritual progress, competitive comparison on the path.

Somatic (body): Physical tension around virtuous others, stress from spiritual comparison.

Emotional current: Jealousy of virtue, resentment of others' progress, spiritual envy, bitter comparison.

Cognitive map: Mental competition in spirituality, distorted thinking about progress, agitation from comparison.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul begrudges what it cannot yet celebrate; resentment at another’s goodness reveals the ego measuring itself against everyone.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — sympathetic joy at goodness and merit

When Mudita is established, the yogi feels genuine joy at others' virtue and spiritual progress. This dissolves competitive spiritual envy.

Somatic (body): Body that lights up at others' goodness, healthy joyful response, warm presence.

Emotional current: Genuine emotional joy at virtue, freedom from spiritual envy, warm celebration of goodness.

Cognitive map: Mental celebration of virtue, freedom from comparison about progress, joyful thinking.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul delights in goodness wherever it appears, taking joy in another’s virtue as its own; this gladness quietly erodes the comparing, competing ego.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — idealization, hero-worship that loses discernment

Excessive Mudita can become overlooking faults, refusing to recognize problems, false positivity about everything.

Somatic (body): Inappropriate positive response to harmful situations.

Emotional current: False positivity, inability to feel appropriate concern, emotional bypassing of legitimate issues.

Cognitive map: Inability to recognize problems, distorted positive thinking, denial of legitimate concerns.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul loses discernment in idealization; admiration becomes hero-worship, projecting onto another the light it has not yet claimed in itself.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — reactive outrage, agitation, the urge to punish

Absence of Upeksha creates being constantly disturbed by others' vice, reactive judgment, mental agitation about others' wrongs.

Somatic (body): Stress and tension from reacting to others' wrongs, body in chronic agitation.

Emotional current: Emotional disturbance by others' vice, reactive judgment, agitation about wrongdoing.

Cognitive map: Mental agitation about others' faults, obsessive judgment, mind unable to find peace because of others.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is seized by reactive outrage; the urge to punish reveals how much the world’s agitation still governs the inner state.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — non-disturbance in the face of wrongdoing

When Upeksha is established, the yogi maintains equanimity in presence of unvirtuous behavior. There is wise discernment without reactive disturbance.

Somatic (body): Calm body in presence of harmful others, healthy nervous system, no reactive tension.

Emotional current: Emotional equanimity facing wickedness, freedom from being thrown by others' vice, peace.

Cognitive map: Mental peace despite others' wrong actions, clear discrimination without reactive judgment, serenity.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul rests undisturbed before wrongdoing, neither condoning nor inflamed; equanimity is the highest of the four, the steadiness of one no longer ruled by reaction.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — detachment that slides into apathy or permissiveness

Excessive Upeksha becomes cold indifference, inability to respond to genuine harm, false detachment that enables evil.

Somatic (body): Physical indifference to harm being done, inability to take protective action.

Emotional current: Emotional coldness, inability to feel appropriate concern about harm, false detachment.

Cognitive map: Mental indifference to wrongdoing, inability to discriminate when action needed, dispassion masking avoidance.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul’s stillness slides into cold indifference; detachment hardens into a refusal to care, mistaking apathy for peace.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — chronic fear, anxiety, paralysis

Absence of Abhayam manifests as chronic fear, anxiety, paralysis in face of challenges, and inability to live fully due to constant apprehension.

Somatic (body): Chronic stress response and adrenal exhaustion, tension and tightness in body, freeze responses to ordinary situations, anxiety-related physical symptoms (panic, palpitations).

Emotional current: Chronic anxiety and fearfulness, emotional paralysis, inability to face challenges, constant apprehension, emotional cowardice, avoidance of life.

Cognitive map: Anxious and fearful thinking patterns, mental paralysis when facing decisions, catastrophizing, inability to think clearly under pressure, mental contraction from fear.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul lives walled in by dread; chronic fear is the precise sign of identification with the perishable.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — courage rooted in awareness

When Abhayam is present, a person acts wisely with awareness of risks but without paralyzing fear. There is courage rooted in awareness of true Self that nothing can ultimately harm.

Somatic (body): Steady physical presence in challenging situations, calm nervous system under threat, healthy adrenal function, body that responds appropriately to actual danger without chronic fear response, courageous physical bearing.

Emotional current: Emotional courage and security, freedom from chronic anxiety, ability to feel fear momentarily without being overwhelmed, emotional grounding in deeper Self, courageous engagement with life.

Cognitive map: Mental clarity in face of challenges, ability to think clearly under pressure, freedom from anxious thinking, courageous decision-making, wisdom about what truly threatens versus apparent threats.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul rests in what cannot be harmed; fearlessness is the natural fragrance of a self that has begun to know its own indestructibility.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — reckless bravado, denial of real risk

Excessive 'fearlessness' without wisdom becomes reckless bravado, denial of legitimate threats, and inability to protect oneself or others appropriately.

Somatic (body): Reckless physical behavior, ignoring legitimate physical dangers, unnecessary physical harm, dismissing physical signals of threat.

Emotional current: False bravado masking actual fear, emotional dismissal of legitimate concerns, inability to acknowledge vulnerability, emotional denial.

Cognitive map: Mental dismissal of real risks, poor judgment about danger, inability to think strategically about threats, recklessness disguised as courage.

Inner presence (psychic): Fearlessness hardens into recklessness; the soul mistakes the suppression of healthy caution for true courage.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — harm in thought, word, and deed

Lack of Ahimsa results in violence, cruelty, harm to others, aggressive behavior, disrespect for life, and inner conflict and guilt.

Somatic (body): Aggressive and violent physical behavior, poor health from violent living, tension and aggression in body, disease from harmful karma, physical harm to others.

Emotional current: Cruelty and lack of compassion, anger and aggression, emotional hardness, guilt and shame from harming others, emotional isolation from damaged relationships, inner conflict.

Cognitive map: Violent and harmful thinking patterns, disrespect for life, lack of conscience, inability to consider impact of actions, mental disturbance from causing harm, low consciousness.

Inner presence (psychic): Harm flows freely from a soul that still sees others as separate; violence in thought, word, or deed is separateness in action.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — harmlessness that still protects what is right

When Ahimsa is balanced, a person avoids unnecessary harm, acts with compassion, stands up for what is right without cruelty, protects the vulnerable, and lives peacefully without aggression.

Somatic (body): Gentle and non-aggressive body language, peaceful physical presence, absence of violence or aggression, good physical health from peaceful living, kind and caring touch.

Emotional current: Compassion and caring for all beings, emotional warmth and kindness, emotional peace and non-reactivity, ability to set boundaries without violence, emotional strength combined with gentleness.

Cognitive map: Thinking that harms no one, mindful and conscious speech, awareness of impact of words and thoughts, wisdom in addressing injustice without harm, mental peace from non-harming.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul wishes harm to nothing; non-violence is the conduct of one who has begun to see the same Self everywhere.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — passivity that permits harm by inaction

Excessive Ahimsa can lead to passivity, inability to establish boundaries, allowing oneself to be harmed, and inability to take necessary protective action.

Somatic (body): Weakness and inability to defend, poor health from allowing harm, passive body language, physical vulnerability, inability to take protective action.

Emotional current: Emotional doormat quality, inability to feel appropriate anger at injustice, emotional suppression and victimhood, emotional weakness and ineffectualness, emotional suffering from passivity.

Cognitive map: Passive thinking and lack of discernment, inability to recognize injustice, mental weakness and indecision, inability to protect others even when necessary, mental paralysis in face of harm.

Inner presence (psychic): Non-violence taken to extremity paralyzes action; the soul, afraid of all harm, cannot perform even necessary duty.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — reactivity, the short fuse, the flooded mind

Absence of Akrodha manifests as chronic anger, rage reactions, hostility, and constant agitation that destroys relationships and well-being.

Somatic (body): Chronic stress response, high blood pressure, cardiovascular disease, muscular tension, stress-related illness, body in constant fight mode.

Emotional current: Chronic anger and rage, emotional volatility, hostility toward others, emotional reactivity to small triggers, inability to feel peace, bitter resentment.

Cognitive map: Mental obsession with grievances, revenge fantasies, distorted thinking from anger, inability to see clearly, cognitive impairment from rage, vengeful planning.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is repeatedly hijacked; chronic reactivity hands its peace to whatever happens to provoke it.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — calm that can still register injustice

When Akrodha is present, a person can recognize injustice and respond firmly without being consumed by destructive anger. There is the capacity for righteous response without the bondage of rage.

Somatic (body): Relaxed physical state, healthy nervous system, absence of tension from suppressed rage, good cardiovascular health, steady physical presence even when challenged, calm body language.

Emotional current: Emotional equanimity that doesn't suppress feelings, ability to feel disappointment or frustration without being consumed, emotional clarity, capacity to address wrongs from peace rather than rage.

Cognitive map: Clear thinking unclouded by rage, ability to discriminate appropriate response from reactive aggression, mental peace, wisdom in addressing injustice, freedom from revenge thinking.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul meets provocation without being seized by it; freedom from anger is the spaciousness in which a clear response can arise.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — suppressed anger, false serenity

Excessive Akrodha as suppression of all anger leads to denied feelings, inability to assert oneself, enabling of harm, and eventual explosive release.

Somatic (body): Tension from suppressed anger, stress-related illness, physical symptoms of held emotion, eventual physical breakdown, somatic symptoms.

Emotional current: Suppressed anger that doesn't process, inability to feel or express legitimate anger, emotional dishonesty, allowing harm without response.

Cognitive map: Mental suppression of legitimate grievances, denial of valid responses, false peace masking inner turmoil, inability to think clearly about wrongs.

Inner presence (psychic): Equanimity curdles into passivity; the soul suppresses even the clean fire that should meet genuine injustice.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — senses as masters, the ungoverned life

Absence of Damah creates slavery to senses, inability to control sensory cravings, addiction, hedonistic excess, and unconscious living.

Somatic (body): Physical addiction and excess, sensory overload, body controlled by cravings, health damaged by sensory indulgence, exhausted nervous system.

Emotional current: Emotional slavery to sensory pleasures, inability to resist temptations, emotional volatility based on sensory input, compulsive seeking.

Cognitive map: Mental obsession with sensory objects, inability to direct attention, scattered mind controlled by senses, addiction patterns.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is enslaved by its own senses; without restraint it is pulled wherever stimulation leads.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — mastered senses serving a chosen life

When Damah is present, a person enjoys sensory experiences without being controlled by them. There is conscious choice in engagement with sense objects.

Somatic (body): Healthy sense organs functioning naturally, appropriate sensory engagement, body that responds to conscious direction, vitality from balanced sensory life.

Emotional current: Emotional freedom from sensory compulsion, ability to enjoy pleasure without enslavement, contentment regardless of sensory input.

Cognitive map: Mental capacity to direct senses consciously, freedom from sensory obsession, wisdom about appropriate sensory engagement, focused attention.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul governs the senses wisely; self-control is not war on the senses but their right ordering under a higher aim.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — rigid repression, joyless control

Excessive Damah becomes harsh sensory denial, rejection of legitimate pleasures, sensory deprivation, and rigid suppression of natural senses.

Somatic (body): Sensory deprivation, atrophied sensory function from disuse, physical rigidity, harsh denial of physical pleasures, ascetic excess.

Emotional current: Emotional suppression of natural pleasure, harsh inner discipline, joylessness, emotional rigidity around pleasure.

Cognitive map: Rigid mental control that suppresses rather than transforms, harsh judgment of sensory engagement, inability to enjoy normal life.

Inner presence (psychic): Control tightens into mortification; the soul punishes its own instrument and mistakes suppression for mastery.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Over-corrected — social ineptitude; unable to observe ordinary social convention

Complete absence of any social performance might manifest as inability to navigate social conventions, naive disclosure inappropriate to context.

Somatic (body): Inability to maintain even appropriate social composure, raw physical expression in inappropriate contexts.

Emotional current: Inappropriate emotional disclosure, inability to maintain composure when needed, raw expression of all feelings regardless of context.

Cognitive map: Inability to filter expression appropriately, no awareness of social context, mental rigidity about full disclosure.

Inner presence (psychic): The pendulum overshoots into social ineptitude; unable to observe even ordinary convention, the soul mistakes awkward bluntness for authenticity.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — transparency; image and reality aligned

When Dambha is absent and replaced with Arjavam (straightforwardness), a person's inner reality matches their outer presentation. There is integrity without false display.

Somatic (body): Natural and authentic physical presence, body that matches inner state, healthy nervous system from integration, no exhaustion from maintaining facades.

Emotional current: Emotional authenticity, freedom from emotional pretense, genuine feelings expressed appropriately, integrity in emotional expression.

Cognitive map: Mental honesty, freedom from self-deception, wisdom about appropriate display versus hiding, integrated thinking aligned with action.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul lets image and reality coincide; with nothing to perform, it can simply be what it is.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — full performance, the curated false self

Severe Dambha creates a life of constant performance—every action is for show. The inner self becomes lost in the maintained facade. This is the excessive/dominant state.

Somatic (body): Constant exhaustion from performing, body language always for show, stress-related illness from pretense, disconnect between body's actual needs and presented state.

Emotional current: Emotional performance rather than feeling, loss of authentic emotional life, exhaustion from emotional theater, emotional emptiness beneath display.

Cognitive map: Mental energy consumed by maintaining appearances, sophisticated rationalizations for hypocrisy, distorted self-perception, mental compartmentalization.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul disappears into its own performance; every act staged for an audience, the inner self starving beneath the mask.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — hardness, indifference to suffering

Absence of Daya creates emotional coldness, indifference to suffering, inability to feel for others, and selfish disregard for those in need.

Somatic (body): Closed and tight chest area, restricted heart and breathing, physical coldness in presence of suffering, hard physical presence toward others.

Emotional current: Emotional coldness and indifference, inability to feel others' pain, hardness of heart, selfish emotional preoccupation, lack of care.

Cognitive map: Mental dismissal of others' suffering, rationalizations for not helping, selfish thinking, inability to consider others' experiences, callous reasoning.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul contracts into cold indifference; the closed heart marks a self still defended against the world.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — warm care that can also act

When Daya is present, a person feels genuine empathy and acts to help others without being overwhelmed or codependent. There is heartful care combined with wisdom about how to truly help.

Somatic (body): Open heart center, warm physical presence, healthy heart and circulation, gentle physical contact with others, body that expresses care, healthy nervous system.

Emotional current: Genuine empathy and emotional warmth, ability to feel others' suffering without absorption, emotional generosity, healing emotional presence, balanced emotional engagement.

Cognitive map: Wisdom about how to help versus enable, clear thinking about others' true needs, mental discernment in compassion, ability to maintain perspective while caring.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul feels the suffering of others as not finally other; compassion is the felt recognition of a shared being.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — over-identification, enabling, burnout

Excessive Daya without wisdom becomes codependent rescuing, emotional overwhelm from others' pain, enabling, and loss of personal boundaries.

Somatic (body): Physical exhaustion from over-helping, neglect of own physical needs, body weighed down by others' burdens, compassion fatigue.

Emotional current: Emotional overwhelm from absorbing others' pain, codependent rescuing, inability to set boundaries, emotional martyrdom, suffering for others to one's own detriment.

Cognitive map: Inability to see when help becomes enabling, mental confusion about appropriate response, loss of perspective, identification with others' suffering.

Inner presence (psychic): Compassion without boundaries drowns the helper; the soul loses its footing in another’s pain and can no longer act.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — shamelessness, the loss of conscience

Absence of Hrih manifests as shamelessness, inability to feel proper restraint, crude behavior, and lack of inner moral compass.

Somatic (body): Crude physical behavior, lack of physical boundaries, inappropriate physical conduct, disrespect for own and others' physical dignity.

Emotional current: Shamelessness and crudeness, inability to feel appropriate shame, lack of moral feeling, emotional coarseness.

Cognitive map: No internal sense of right action, mental rationalizations for any behavior, inability to discriminate dignified from undignified, missing conscience.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul loses its inner compass; shamelessness is the inability to feel the quiet ‘no’ of conscience before wrong.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — a wholesome conscience and humility

When Hrih is present, a person naturally avoids actions that would compromise dignity or violate ethical principles. There is innate sense of propriety without false prudishness.

Somatic (body): Naturally modest physical presence, appropriate physical boundaries, dignified body language, healthy sense of physical privacy.

Emotional current: Innate sense of emotional propriety, ability to feel appropriate shame when warranted, healthy moral compass, emotional dignity.

Cognitive map: Clear inner sense of right and wrong action, mental restraint based on values, wisdom about appropriate conduct, conscience that guides without crippling.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul carries a healthy reverence — the capacity for proper shame that keeps action aligned with conscience.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — shame, self-erasure, chronic embarrassment

Excessive Hrih becomes false modesty, shame-based existence, inability to act due to fear of criticism, and chronic self-judgment.

Somatic (body): Physical shrinking and hiding, inability to express oneself physically, shame-based body language, physical paralysis from shame.

Emotional current: Chronic shame and self-judgment, paralyzing modesty, emotional contraction, inability to celebrate or express joy, false humility.

Cognitive map: Constant self-criticism, mental shame, inability to act for fear of being seen, paralysis from concern about judgment, distorted self-image.

Inner presence (psychic): Modesty collapses into crippling self-effacement; the soul shrinks from its own legitimate light, mistaking smallness for virtue.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Over-suppressed — desire forcibly repressed, breeding frustration, resentment, and inner conflict

Suppression of Kama without understanding leads to frustration, resentment, inner conflict, and eventual explosive release.

Somatic (body): Lack of vitality from suppressed desires, physical tension and rigidity, sexual dysfunction or issues, poor appetite, weak energy, eventual health problems from repression.

Emotional current: Emotional frustration and resentment, suppressed emotions seeking outlet, emotional coldness and disconnection, emotional explosiveness when barriers break, emotional emptiness and joylessness.

Cognitive map: Mental preoccupation with suppressed desires, mental rigidity and control, intrusive thoughts breaking through barriers, mental anxiety and tension, eventual mental breakdown or explosion.

Inner presence (psychic): Desire forcibly repressed turns inward as frustration; the soul at war with its own wanting finds the denied energy curdling into resentment.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — desire directed toward the worthy

When Kama is balanced and acknowledged, a person can pursue legitimate pleasures and desires without being enslaved by them. Desires are fulfilled appropriately while maintaining spiritual focus.

Somatic (body): Good sensory enjoyment and appreciation, healthy appetite and sexuality, pleasure in food, movement, and physical experiences, vitality from enjoying life, good health from balanced satisfaction.

Emotional current: Joy and pleasure in appropriate measure, emotional satisfaction from fulfilling desires, emotional richness and engagement, emotional freedom and spontaneity, balanced attachments.

Cognitive map: Clear understanding of desires and their nature, wisdom in pursuing desires, ability to enjoy without obsession, mental clarity about what truly satisfies, intentional choice of desires to pursue.

Inner presence (psychic): Desire directed toward the worthy becomes fuel; the soul lets longing pull it toward what genuinely nourishes.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — insatiable craving, the never-enough

Excessive Kama leads to addiction, obsession with pleasure, loss of self-control, damaged relationships, financial ruin, and health problems.

Somatic (body): Addiction to sensory pleasures, poor health from excess (obesity, disease), depleted energy, addiction to alcohol/drugs/sex, physical deterioration, chronic illness from excess.

Emotional current: Emotional reactivity and obsession, emotional instability and mood swings, emotional neediness and dependence, damaged relationships from excess, emotional emptiness beneath pursuit of pleasure.

Cognitive map: Obsessive thinking about desired objects, mental slavery to desires, poor judgment and decision-making, inability to think of anything else, loss of self-control, diminished mental clarity.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul is consumed by insatiable craving; the Gita names kāma the eternal enemy, the fire no fuel can satisfy.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Over-suppressed — anger denied until it curdles into resentment and the inability to register genuine wrong

Complete suppression of all anger (rather than transcendence) leads to inability to recognize wrongs, enabling injustice, suppressed rage erupting, and lack of legitimate self-protection.

Somatic (body): Tension from suppressed anger, eventual explosive release, stress-related illness from holding emotions, somatic symptoms.

Emotional current: Suppressed anger creating bitterness, inability to feel legitimate frustration, emotional dishonesty, inability to set protective boundaries.

Cognitive map: Denial of valid grievances, mental suppression of legitimate concerns, eventual mental breakdown from accumulated suppression.

Inner presence (psychic): Anger denied festers into resentment; the soul that cannot feel honest indignation also loses the power to recognize and name genuine wrong.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — the energy of anger transmuted into clear action

When Krodha is transcended, a person responds to frustration with wisdom rather than reactive anger. There is capacity for righteous response without being consumed by rage.

Somatic (body): Calm nervous system even when frustrated, healthy stress response, body in equilibrium, no chronic tension from suppressed anger.

Emotional current: Emotional equanimity in face of frustration, ability to feel disappointment without rage, healthy boundaries without aggression, emotional maturity.

Cognitive map: Clear thinking when frustrated, freedom from revenge thinking, wisdom about appropriate response, mental peace despite obstacles.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul transmutes the heat of anger into clear, righteous energy; the fire warms and lights rather than destroys.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — rage, the flooded and destructive mind

Excessive Krodha is rage that destroys discrimination and consciousness. According to Gita, it directly leads to delusion, loss of memory, and ultimately destruction of intellect.

Somatic (body): Frequent rage states, high blood pressure, cardiovascular damage, stress-related illness, muscular tension, depleted adrenals, physical aggression.

Emotional current: Emotional rage and hostility, inability to control emotional reactions, bitterness, emotional volatility, emotional cruelty when angry.

Cognitive map: Loss of mental discrimination during anger, distorted thinking, vengeful planning, inability to think clearly, mental destruction from rage cycles.

Inner presence (psychic): Rage drowns discrimination; in its grip the soul loses the very consciousness through which it might choose — the road the Gita calls ruin.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — hardness, abrasiveness, the bruising manner

Absence of Märdavam creates harshness, roughness in conduct, hurtful manner of interaction, and inability to soften when appropriate.

Somatic (body): Harsh physical contact, rough body language, abrasive physical presence, ungentle touch.

Emotional current: Emotional harshness and roughness, inability to soften emotionally, emotional brusqueness, lack of warmth.

Cognitive map: Harsh thinking patterns, rough mental engagement, inability to consider sensitivities, blunt and unrefined mental approach.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul roughens; harshness in manner and speech is the surface sign of an inner brittleness.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — strength expressed as gentleness

When Märdavam is present, a person interacts with others gently and kindly while maintaining strength. There is softness without weakness, gentleness without timidity.

Somatic (body): Gentle physical touch and presence, soft body language, peaceful physical demeanor, healthy nervous system from peaceful engagement.

Emotional current: Emotional softness and warmth, kind emotional expression, gentle approach to others, emotional tenderness combined with strength.

Cognitive map: Gentle thinking patterns, mental refinement, sensitive consideration of others, thoughtful and considerate mental engagement.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul meets the world with a softness that is not weakness; gentleness is strength that has no need to harden.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — softness without spine, conflict-avoidance

Excessive Märdavam can become weak softness, inability to take firm action when needed, over-sensitivity, and ineffective gentleness in face of harshness.

Somatic (body): Physical weakness and inability to assert, lack of physical strength when needed, over-yielding to others' physical assertions.

Emotional current: Emotional weakness disguised as gentleness, inability to be firm, over-sensitivity, lack of healthy assertiveness.

Cognitive map: Mental weakness and inability to take strong positions, over-accommodating thinking, lack of mental firmness when needed.

Inner presence (psychic): Gentleness slides into spinelessness; the soul cannot hold a needed edge and is shaped by whoever pushes hardest.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Over-corrected — ineffectual softness; unable to speak with the firmness a situation needs

Complete absence of any firmness in speech becomes ineffectual softness, inability to communicate hard truths, and weakness disguised as kindness.

Somatic (body): Physical inability to assert through voice and presence, weakness in communication, lack of conviction in speech.

Emotional current: Emotional inability to express hard truths, false sweetness, inability to be firm when needed, emotional cowardice.

Cognitive map: Mental inability to express direct positions, evasive communication, lack of clarity in expression.

Inner presence (psychic): Softness becomes ineffectual; unable to speak the firm word a moment requires, the soul lets necessary truths go unsaid.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — speech that is firm but never cruel

When Pärusyam is absent and Anudvegakaram (non-hurtful speech) is present, a person speaks truth without harshness. Communication is firm when needed but never cruel.

Somatic (body): Calm physical demeanor, gentle physical presence, healthy nervous system, no chronic tension from aggressive interactions.

Emotional current: Emotional warmth and gentleness in communication, ability to be firm without cruelty, emotional regulation in speech, peaceful relationships.

Cognitive map: Mental refinement in expression, thoughtful word choice, wisdom about impact of speech, considerate communication.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul speaks truth that does not wound; firm and kind at once, its words carry weight without leaving injury.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — cutting, contemptuous, wounding speech

Excessive Pärusyam manifests as habitual cruelty in speech, rough manner, hurtful communication patterns, and verbal aggression that damages relationships.

Somatic (body): Aggressive body language, harsh facial expressions, tense nervous system from constant verbal aggression, physical signs of inner aggression.

Emotional current: Emotional cruelty in expression, inability to communicate without hurting, damaged relationships, emotional aggression, hostile communication patterns.

Cognitive map: Harsh thinking patterns, cruel mental engagement, inability to consider impact of words, aggressive mental approach to others, contempt.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul cuts with its tongue; cruel speech reveals a hardness within that wounds others and, finally, the speaker.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — deceit, evasion, the divided self

Absence of Satya manifests as habitual lying, deception, self-deception, manipulation, and disconnection from reality. It creates fragmentation and suffering.

Somatic (body): Body language that contradicts words, physical signs of stress from constant lies, exhausted nervous system from maintaining pretense, somatic symptoms.

Emotional current: Emotional deception and self-deception, inability to feel authentically, emotional disconnection from reality, exhaustion from emotional facades.

Cognitive map: Habitual lying and rationalization, mental disconnection from truth, fragmented thinking, deceptive mental patterns, self-deception.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul fragments itself through deceit; each lie divides what is from what is shown, and self-deception is the deepest division of all.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — honesty aligned with kindness

When Satya is present, a person speaks and lives in accordance with truth while exercising wisdom about how and when to express it. Truth is delivered with kindness and care.

Somatic (body): Body language consistent with words, nervous system at ease from lack of pretense, healthy physical presence from authenticity, no need to hide.

Emotional current: Emotional honesty with self and others, freedom from emotional pretense, emotional integrity, authentic expression of feelings, peace from authenticity.

Cognitive map: Clear honest thinking, mental integrity, freedom from rationalization and self-deception, wisdom in expression, mental clarity from authenticity.

Inner presence (psychic): The soul aligns word, thought, and deed; truthfulness is less a rule than the transparency of a being with nothing to hide.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — brutal honesty, truth as a weapon

Excessive Satya without wisdom becomes brutal honesty, hurtful truth-telling, inappropriate disclosure, and harsh truth delivered without compassion.

Somatic (body): Harsh physical presence, blunt body language, inappropriate physical disclosure.

Emotional current: Emotional brutality disguised as honesty, hurtful truth-telling, inability to be tactful, harshness in disclosure.

Cognitive map: Rigid insistence on truth-telling without context, inability to recognize when discretion serves higher truth, mental harshness about facts.

Inner presence (psychic): Truth wielded without compassion becomes a weapon; the soul mistakes brutal candor for honesty and wounds in the name of the real.

descriptions.deficiency:
## Deficient — inner and outer impurity, the muddied life

Absence of Shaucham creates physical uncleanliness, neglect of hygiene, internal moral pollution, corrupt motives, and toxic mental states.

Somatic (body): Poor hygiene, dirty body and environment, disease from uncleanliness, physical deterioration, neglect of body.

Emotional current: Corrupt motives, toxic emotional patterns, emotional contamination, allowing destructive feelings, emotional uncleanness.

Cognitive map: Corrupt and impure thinking, toxic mental patterns, allowing harmful thoughts, mental contamination.

Inner presence (psychic): Neglect dulls the instrument; impurity of body and mind clouds the very clarity through which the soul would see.
descriptions.balance:
## Balanced — cleanliness of body, speech, and intent

When Shaucham is present, a person maintains appropriate hygiene and cleanliness while cultivating inner purity of mind and motive. There is natural cleanliness without obsession.

Somatic (body): Clean and well-maintained body, healthy hygiene practices, clean environment, body that radiates wellness, healthy elimination.

Emotional current: Pure motives and emotional clarity, freedom from corrupting emotions, emotional cleanness, absence of toxic feelings.

Cognitive map: Pure thoughts and clean mental patterns, freedom from corrupted thinking, mental clarity, ability to think clean thoughts.

Inner presence (psychic): Outer and inner cleanliness mirror each other; purity is the soul keeping its instrument clear enough to reflect the real.
descriptions.excess:
## In excess — obsessive purity, scrupulosity

Excessive Shaucham becomes obsessive cleanliness, germphobia, perfectionism about purity, and rigid judgment of others' impurity.

Somatic (body): OCD-like cleaning behaviors, skin damage from over-washing, anxiety about contamination, inability to engage with normal world due to cleanliness obsession.

Emotional current: Disgust at any imperfection, emotional rigidity about purity, anxiety about contamination, judgment of others.

Cognitive map: Obsessive thinking about cleanliness, inability to engage with messy reality, perfectionism, mental rigidity about purity standards.

Inner presence (psychic): Purity becomes obsession; the soul’s hunger for cleanness turns into anxious ritual and contempt for ordinary embodiment.
